Perform a multi-reference image edit using three reference images. Image 1 defines the subject’s character identity and head design. The polar bear character from Image 1 must remain exactly the same, with no changes to head shape, snout proportions, eye shape and spacing, ear placement, facial expression style, fur color, fur volume, or overall character identity. Maintain the exact facial geometry appropriate to a polar bear character. No identity shift of any kind. Do not humanize the face or alter species-defining features. Preserve the bear’s fur texture and coloration exactly as shown in Image 1. Do not introduce markings or features not present in Image 1. Image 2 defines the body pose and camera framing. Place the polar bear character from Image 1 into the exact pose and framing from Image 2. Maintain the same camera angle, distance, and crop. The pose should be interpreted as a human-style seated portrait posture adapted naturally to a polar bear body. Image 3 defines the outfit. Dress the polar bear character in the outfit from Image 3, adapting the clothing naturally to fit a polar bear body while preserving fabric, fit, color, and details. Replace the background with a clean studio-style solid tan neutral background. Priority order strict: character identity from Image 1 first, pose and framing from Image 2 second, outfit from Image 3 third, background last. Render as a high-quality 3D CGI character image with smooth sculpted fur (not photorealistic fur strands), clean shading, professional studio lighting, and polished cinematic color rendering.
